text.title: ai-in-project-management Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ai-in-project-management as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLM hallucinating task IDs and dates in long-context report generation.
failure-mode.detector: Filled artefact, log, or vendor dashboard exhibits the pattern described in the parent description (e.g. velocity claims from non-calibrated baseline, mixed-model outputs without label, niche-domain breakdowns without per-domain calibration).
failure-mode.repair: Always validate task IDs and dates against the source PM system before including in reports; hallucinated IDs cause stakeholder confusion and erode trust.

failure-mode.description: AI-generated summaries reporting "green" status that hides a critical qualitative flag.
failure-mode.detector: Filled artefact, log, or vendor dashboard exhibits the pattern described in the parent description (e.g. velocity claims from non-calibrated baseline, mixed-model outputs without label, niche-domain breakdowns without per-domain calibration).
failure-mode.repair: AI risk scoring is only as good as its structured inputs; qualitative concerns from team members (unstated in tickets) do not appear in data-driven summaries.

failure-mode.description: Pinning to no model version for digest agents.
failure-mode.detector: Filled artefact, log, or vendor dashboard exhibits the pattern described in the parent description (e.g. velocity claims from non-calibrated baseline, mixed-model outputs without label, niche-domain breakdowns without per-domain calibration).
failure-mode.repair: A Haiku upgrade can change summary tone noticeably — pin the model; digest style should be consistent across reports.
